entities.name: INDIAN PEANUT CLUMP VIRUS-H
entities.description: A strain of the Indian peanut clump virus used as the source of the coat protein gene.

entities.name: COAT PROTEIN GENE
entities.description: The gene encoding the coat protein (CP) of the virus, responsible for the assembly of virus-like particles.

entities.name: NICOTIANA BENTHAMIANA
entities.description: A plant species used as a host for expressing the coat protein gene.

entities.name: ESCHERICHIA COLI
entities.description: A bacterial species used as a host for expressing the coat protein gene.

entities.name: TRANSFORMATION
entities.description: The process of introducing foreign genetic material into a cell using vectors.

entities.name: PROKII
entities.description: A vector used for transformation into plant cells.

entities.name: PET
entities.description: A vector used for transformation into bacterial cells.

entities.name: VIRUS-LIKE PARTICLES
entities.description: Protein shells that mimic the structure of a virus, formed by the assembly of the coat protein.

entities.name: IMMUNOCAPTURE RT-PCR
entities.description: A technique used to detect and amplify specific RNA sequences within captured particles.

entities.name: ENCAPSIDATION
entities.description: The process by which a viral genome or RNA is enclosed within a protein shell.

entities.name: 5′-TERMINAL UNTRANSLATED SEQUENCE
entities.description: A non-coding sequence at the 5' end of the viral RNA.

entities.name: ORIGIN OF ASSEMBLY MOTIF
entities.description: A specific sequence or motif that triggers the assembly of the viral capsid.

entities.name: INDIAN PEANUT CLUMP VIRUS-L
entities.description: A serologically distinct strain of the Indian peanut clump virus used for inoculation.
